skill.name: azure-observability
skill.description: Guides Azure observability work across Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Log Analytics, alerts, workbooks, KQL, and Azure SDKs.
skill.summary: Use Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Log Analytics, alerts, and SDK references to investigate Azure telemetry.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, network, environment, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found documentation examples rather than executable skill code. The only residual risks are that users may run Azure CLI or SDK examples against live resources and should protect connection strings. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or confirmed malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Execution
low_findings.description: The static command findings are documentation examples for Azure CLI, MCP command names, package installation, and SDK quick starts. The files do not define executable scripts or invoke commands automatically, so these are false positives with normal user-action risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown tables, fenced examples, package install commands, or SDK snippets. No executable wrapper, shell interpolation, or automatic command invocation was found.

low_findings.title: Azure Documentation Links and Endpoint Pattern Are Benign
low_findings.description: The network findings are Azure documentation links and a placeholder Azure Monitor ingestion endpoint pattern. They do not send data, collect credentials, or direct traffic to an unknown third party.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to Microsoft Learn pages or an Azure Monitor service endpoint template. No code path performs an outbound request from the skill.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Example Reads a Connection String
low_findings.description: The TypeScript quick start shows reading APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING from process.env. This is a standard Azure configuration pattern, but users should treat the value as sensitive and avoid exposing it in prompts or logs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable access is real, but it appears only in an SDK documentation snippet. There is no evidence that the skill itself reads, stores, or transmits environment data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections map to ordinary text and KQL sort direction words, not cryptographic API usage. No DES, MD5, SHA1, or similar algorithm implementation was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are description text, KQL ordering clauses, and SDK reference prose. They contain no cryptographic operation or security-sensitive hashing behavior.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Dismissed After Review
low_findings.description: The combined command, network, and credential heuristic is not supported by semantic review. The evidence is split across markdown examples, Microsoft documentation links, and one standard environment-variable configuration sample.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The same suspicious categories exist only as passive guidance, not as coordinated behavior. No evidence found of obfuscation, hidden network calls, or automated credential access.

content.value_statement: Azure teams need fast access to telemetry guidance across metrics, logs, traces, alerts, and workbooks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise Azure Monitor references and practical investigation prompts.
actual_capabilities: Maps Azure observability tasks to Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Log Analytics, alerts, and workbooks.
Provides Azure CLI examples for listing workspaces, querying logs, listing alerts, and reading metrics.
Includes common KQL examples for recent errors, request performance, and resource usage.
Summarizes Azure SDK setup for Python, TypeScript, Java, and .NET observability workflows.
Recommends monitoring practices for errors, performance, infrastructure, security logs, and cost alerts.
limitations: It is a reference skill and does not provision Azure resources by itself.
It assumes users already have Azure access, resource identifiers, and permissions.
SDK notes are condensed and link to deeper skill references for advanced patterns.
It does not replace live incident response judgment or organization-specific alert policy.

use_cases.title: Investigate Production Incidents
use_cases.description: Use KQL, metrics, and Application Insights guidance to narrow errors, latency, and dependency issues during an incident.

use_cases.title: Add Application Telemetry
use_cases.description: Find the right OpenTelemetry and Application Insights setup path for Python, TypeScript, Java, or .NET services.

use_cases.title: Design Monitoring Coverage
use_cases.description: Plan which Azure service, metric, log, alert, or workbook should cover each operational concern.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Azure Observability Service
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to monitor an Azure workload. Help me choose between Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Log Analytics, alerts, and workbooks for my goal.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Basic KQL Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create KQL queries to find recent application errors, slow requests, and high CPU resources for the last hour.

prompt_templates.title: Plan SDK Instrumentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend Azure Monitor SDK setup steps for my service language, authentication approach, sampling needs, and production telemetry requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Observability Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Azure observability design for coverage gaps across logs, metrics, traces, alerts, dashboards, retention, cost, and secure ingestion.

output_examples.input: Find the best Azure service for tracing a web API and querying failures.
output: Use Application Insights for request tracing and dependency visibility.
Use Log Analytics with KQL to query exceptions, failed requests, and related platform logs.
Add alerts for failure rate, availability, and latency thresholds.

output_examples.input: Help me investigate high CPU on an Azure resource.
output: Start with Azure Monitor metrics for the resource and a one-hour time range.
Check Log Analytics for correlated activity, deployment, and error events.
Summarize affected resources, time windows, and next validation steps.

output_examples.input: Plan telemetry for a new Node.js service in Azure.
output: Use Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for traces, metrics, and logs.
Store the Application Insights connection string as a protected environment setting.
Set sampling, role names, and graceful shutdown before production rollout.

best_practices: Use managed identity or Azure identity libraries instead of embedding credentials in prompts or files.
Keep KQL queries scoped by time, projection, and aggregation to reduce cost and noise.
Validate alert thresholds with real workload behavior before enabling broad notifications.
anti_patterns: Do not paste Application Insights connection strings or tenant secrets into chat context.
Do not run broad log queries without time bounds during active incidents.
Do not treat sample CLI commands as production automation without review and access controls.

faq.question: Does this skill run Azure commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides reference commands and guidance that a user or approved tool can execute separately.

faq.question: Which Azure services does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Log Analytics, alerts, workbooks, KQL, and observability SDK references.

faq.question: Can it help with KQL queries?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes common KQL patterns for errors, request performance, and resource usage.

faq.question: Does it include SDK guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It has condensed references for Python, TypeScript, Java, and .NET observability SDKs.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with secrets?
faq.answer: Use it without sharing secrets. Keep connection strings in protected environment variables or Azure-managed identity flows.

faq.question: Can it design a complete monitoring strategy?
faq.answer: It can guide service selection and coverage planning, but final thresholds and retention policies need environment-specific review.
